summary: Claude Code (Anthropic) is a Node/TypeScript terminal-first coding assistant (research preview v0.2.8). It provides a React Ink TUI, built-in tools (bash, file edit, grep, glob, notebook tools), Anthropic Claude API integration, permission/trust dialogs, ripgrep-based fast search, MCP (Model Context Protocol) client support, telemetry (Statsig) and Sentry error reporting with sourcemap-related debug metadata. Repository inspected: ../claude-code-sourcemap (README.md, LICENSE.md, cli.mjs, src/* entrypoints/services/tools).

features.name: CLI entrypoint
features.description: Node-based CLI (cli.mjs / src/entrypoints/cli.tsx) to launch TUI or run one-shot prompts.

features.name: React Ink TUI
features.description: Full-screen terminal UI (components/screens) including REPL, onboarding, logs, dialogs, and streaming chat.

features.name: Anthropic Claude integration
features.description: services/claude.ts wraps Anthropic SDK and provides streaming LLM interactions and error handling.

features.name: Tools system
features.description: Modular tools (BashTool, FileEditTool, FileRead/Write, Grep/Glob, Notebook tools, MCPTool, etc.) that the agent can call.

features.name: Persistent sessions and logging
features.description: Message logs, message recovery utilities, and local storage of conversation logs.

features.name: Permissions & Trust flow
features.description: Interactive trust dialog and permission gating for file writes/shell commands with explicit UI prompts.

features.name: Ripgrep integration
features.description: Bundled ripgrep binary and utils/ripgrep.ts for fast repository search.

features.name: Bash persistent shell
features.description: PersistentShell utility and BashTool to execute shell commands with banned-command checks and output formatting.

features.name: MCP client/server support
features.description: Model Context Protocol client/server hooks to extend tools via external MCP servers.

features.name: OAuth & API key flows
features.description: Console OAuth flows to create and store API keys and approve custom keys.

features.name: Binary & structured feedback
features.description: Sampling and logging for binary feedback used in product experiments (components/binary-feedback).

features.name: Auto-updater helpers
features.description: utils/autoUpdater for checking/installing newer versions or suggesting global install steps.

features.name: Statsig feature gates
features.description: Statsig integration for feature gating and experiments.

features.name: Sentry & sourcemap handling
features.description: Sentry integration (services/sentry + bundled Sentry client) includes debug_meta/images with type 'sourcemap' for event mapping.

top_level.note: Pre-built Node bundle CLI with dependencies and Sentry client.
TypeScript sources: components, screens, services, tools, utils.
WASM module used by Ink layout or UI engine.

key_modules.note: Main CLI startup: init Sentry, onboarding, trust dialog, render TUI, set up services.
Anthropic client wrapper: verifyApiKey, query streaming, message conversion, model prompt builders.
Tool implementations for Bash, FileEdit, Grep, Glob, Notebook tools, MCP tool; each tool supplies a prompt descriptor.
MCP server/client management: list servers, run commands, wrap remote tools.
Integration and helper to call the bundled ripgrep binary for fast search.
Sentry init/capture and logic for debug ids and sourcemap attachments inside the build bundle.

integration_points.api_providers: Anthropic (@anthropic-ai/sdk shims), OAuth flows for API key provisioning
integration_points.sourcemap_specific: Sentry integration attaches debug_meta.images of type 'sourcemap' (bundled Sentry code in cli.mjs), repo built with extracted sourcemaps to surface readable stack traces in Sentry.

strengths: Polished terminal UX with onboarding and trust dialogs.
Rich built-in toolset and per-tool prompts for safe tool use.
Bundled ripgrep for fast repo search.
MCP support to extend capabilities via remote servers.
weaknesses: License/redistribution restricted under Anthropic commercial terms (not permissive).
Tightly coupled to Anthropic/Claude (single provider bias).
Large JS bundle and many runtime dependencies (vs Rust static binary), increasing attack surface and operational complexity.
Telemetry collection and external services may conflict with privacy/self-hosting goals.

adoptable_features.rationale: Fast search improves code analysis and context retrieval for large repos.
adoptable_features.implementation: Add ripgrep wrapper in ragent-core tools (spawn binary or include ripgrep vendor), stream results into tool messages; add TUI results panel; tests: wrapper parsing and integration tests. Files: crates/ragent-core/src/tools.rs, ragent-tui UI updates.

adoptable_features.rationale: Prevents accidental destructive changes; improves security UX.
adoptable_features.implementation: Add onboarding/trust dialog in ragent-tui; extend permission checks in ragent-core and add file-edit diff preview; tests: integration tests for permission prompts. Files: ragent-tui/src/dialogs.rs, crates/ragent-core/src/permissions.rs.

adoptable_features.rationale: Better model grounding and safer tool invocation semantics.
adoptable_features.implementation: Extend Tool trait with prompt_description field; ensure prompt builder includes these descriptions. Files: crates/ragent-core/src/tools/mod.rs, crates/ragent-core/src/providers/prompt_builder.rs.

adoptable_features.rationale: Improves product research and UX iteration.
adoptable_features.implementation: Implement local feedback sampling module and optionally server-side ingestion; add UI component to collect binary feedback; tests: local sink and opt-in behavior. Files: crates/ragent-core/src/feedback.rs, ragent-server/some-endpoints.

adoptable_features.rationale: Allows extending tools via remote servers and avoids shipping heavy third-party binaries.
adoptable_features.implementation: Implement MCP client crate to discover remote tools and wrap them as local tools. Files: crates/ragent-core/src/mcp_client.rs, ragent-server endpoints or connector. Tests: mock MCP server integration test.

risks.details: Claude Code is under Anthropic commercial terms; do not copy code or assets without legal review. Mitigation: reimplement features independently and avoid direct source reuse.
Collecting transcripts and telemetry can cause privacy/regulatory concerns. Mitigation: opt-in, local-first storage, redaction, retention policies.
Uploading source maps to Sentry risks exposing source code. Mitigation: restrict uploads, require auth, or avoid uploading sourcemaps from private repos.
Spawning ripgrep or remote MCP or heavy model calls could be resource-intensive. Mitigation: streaming, rate-limits, caching.
MCP/remote tools and shell execution expand attack surface. Mitigation: strong permission gating, signature verification for remote responses, sandboxing.

pocexperiments.name: Ripgrep integration POC
pocexperiments.description: Spawn system ripgrep from ragent, stream results to TUI. Success: significant speed improvement vs naive file walk.

pocexperiments.name: Permission dialog & file-edit diff POC
pocexperiments.description: Add TUI dialog for trust and show unified diff preview before file writes. Success: flows prevent accidental writes in manual testing.

pocexperiments.name: MCP connectivity POC
pocexperiments.description: Stand up mock MCP server and implement client to call remote tool; Success: successful remote tool invocation and streamed results in UI.

license.implications: Restricted reuse; must not copy code or assets into MIT-licensed ragent without legal approval. Use only as inspiration and reimplement features independently.
